name: Productivity/B2B Onboarding
definition_origin: Category pattern crystallized 2018-2024 by the Linear/Notion/Cron/Superhuman/Slack school of opinionated minimal onboarding. Distinct from consumer-wellness story-onboarding doctrine. Origin sources: Karri Saarinen's 'Linear Onboarding' essay (linear.app/blog, 2021-2023); Notion's onboarding teardowns (Mobbin, designerup.co, Reforge); Slack's 2000-message activation finding (Patrick Moran 2014, foundational); Superhuman's 'Game Day' personal-onboarding-call doctrine (Rahul Vohra interview, First Round Review 2018); Monday.com's invite-as-activation pattern; ProductLed.org canon ('PLG playbook', Wes Bush). Codified across Reforge Activation Bootcamp curriculum, Lenny's Newsletter PLG canon, Heap/Amplitude playbooks, ProductLed Institute. Differs from consumer onboarding because: (a) the user often did NOT choose to install (admin invited them), (b) the value is collaborative (network effect), (c) trial economics differ (per-seat licensing, free-tier expansion), (d) the user is at work (low patience for narrative).
core_mechanism: Productivity/B2B onboarding works by removing decisions, not adding them. The dominant mechanism is opinionated empty-state seeding — the app presents a ready-made workspace populated with sensible defaults (Linear's example issues, Notion's templates, Slack's #general channel) so the user reaches first-meaningful-action in <60 seconds. Five reinforcing forces: (1) Cognitive load minimization (Hick's Law) — productivity users have ZERO patience for 33-step questionnaires; every screen of friction tanks D1 retention. (2) Network-effect activation — the canonical aha is multi-user (Slack 2000 messages, Notion 5+ blocks across 2 pages, Monday invite-a-colleague). Solo onboarding succeeds only when team-onboarding follows. (3) Identity preserved, not formed — productivity users already self-identify ('I'm a PM', 'I'm a designer'); onboarding asks role-as-routing-input, NOT as identity formation. (4) Sage/Ruler register dominates — copy is informational, opinionated, confident; NOT 'gentle pacing'. (5) Free-tier as activation engine, paywall deferred — most B2B SaaS don't gate at onboarding; they gate at usage thresholds (Linear 250 issues, Notion blocks, Figma editors).
neuroscience_correlate: Decision-fatigue minimization (prefrontal cortex glucose depletion, Baumeister 2008) — fewer choices preserve cognitive resources for the actual work. Self-efficacy from quick wins (Bandura 1977) — first task completed in 60s primes continued use. Network-effect dopamine — collaborative pings (a teammate replied) trigger social-reward circuits (ventral striatum, social-affiliation). Avoidance of identity-formation pressure — productivity users at work resist consumer-app emotional manipulation as 'unprofessional'.
empirical_evidence: Slack '2000 messages' activation finding (Patrick Moran 2014; foundational PLG literature): teams that hit 2000 messages had 93% retention; below 2000 had ~30%. Slack onboarding optimized to drive teams to that threshold within 10 days. Notion onboarding (designerup.co teardown 2024): minimal 3-screen welcome → role question → templated workspace; achieves D7 free-tier retention ~25-30%. Linear (Karri Saarinen, linear.app/blog 2023): zero-questionnaire onboarding, opinionated empty state with example issues, D7 retention publicly reported >40% for new workspaces. Cron (acquired by Notion 2022): 4-screen onboarding (account → calendar OAuth → keyboard-shortcuts tour → done) — installation-to-first-event-created ~90 seconds. Superhuman 'Game Day' onboarding (1:1 video call 30-min with onboarding specialist, $30/mo product): drove $20M ARR with 80%+ activation; doesn't scale to free tier but proves activation-quality matters more than activation-volume. Monday.com 'invite a colleague' as core activation: teams that invited 3+ users D1 hit 65% D30 retention vs 18% solo (Heap PLG study 2023). Figma onboarding: file-creation as activation, no questionnaire, hit $400M ARR pre-Adobe-deal-attempt. Calendly onboarding: 4-step (account → connect calendar → set availability → share link); 2-min flow. Reforge Activation Bootcamp 2024 cohort data: B2B SaaS onboarding completion correlates more with simplicity than personalization (inverse of consumer wellness). PostHog State of PLG 2024: B2B SaaS median onboarding length 4-6 screens; >10 screens correlates with 35% lower D7 retention.
measurable_outcome_metric: Time-to-first-meaningful-action (target <60 seconds — Linear standard; <90 seconds — Notion). D1 product activation rate (defined per-product: Slack first message, Notion first block edit, Linear first issue, Figma first file). D7 workspace retention. Invite-conversion rate (% of first users who invite a teammate within 7 days). Workspace-to-paid conversion at usage threshold (Linear 250-issue paywall, Figma editor seat). Free-tier expansion rate (% of free workspaces that hit usage threshold). Setup completion rate (% who finish full setup including integrations). NPS at D14 (productivity users churn fast on bad UX).
conversion_lift_benchmark: Slack 2000-messages threshold: 93% retention vs 30% below threshold. Monday invite-a-colleague: 65% D30 vs 18% solo (3.6× lift). Linear opinionated empty state: D7 >40% (vs ~25% Notion templated). PostHog 2024: <6-screen onboarding has 35% higher D7 vs >10-screen. Reforge: first-meaningful-action in <60s correlates with 2.4× D14 retention vs >5min onboarding. Superhuman 'Game Day': 80%+ activation rate (single-call cost ~$200/user, justified by $30/mo + low churn).
engineering_tactics: DEFAULT PATTERN: Frictionless / Deferred Pattern (item 19) extended with role-routing — minimal onboarding (3-6 screens), opinionated empty state seeding (item 21), network-effect-activation prompts. SPECIFIC SEQUENCE: (1) Auth screen — magic link (item 60) or SSO (Google/Microsoft Workspace + SSO for enterprise via Auth0/Okta/WorkOS). NO password by default in 2025-2026 productivity SaaS. (2) Workspace creation OR join-existing-workspace (Notion, Slack, Linear all support both branches; deep-link from invite email routes to join). (3) Role question (4-7 chips: PM / Designer / Engineer / Founder / Marketer / Other) — used for opinionated workspace seeding, NOT identity formation. (4) Workspace seeding — pre-populated example issues (Linear), templates by role (Notion), example pipeline (Asana). NEVER blank empty state. (5) Single primary CTA on first dashboard ('Create your first issue' / 'Start typing'). (6) Invite-a-teammate prompt deferred to first-success (after first issue created, after first block typed, NOT before). (7) Settings/integrations deferred to contextual triggers ('Connect Slack to get notifications' shown when user creates an event). NO paywall at onboarding — gate at usage threshold post-aha. FSM: minimal (XState v5 still useful for resume hydration on cross-device). State persistence: backend (workspace_id is the source of truth; client hydrates). Server-side: workspace_state, role, seeded_templates, invite_status. Web-primary; mobile is companion. SSO + magic-link infrastructure: WorkOS, Stytch, Clerk, Auth0 Universal Login, Supabase Auth. Forbid: 33-step questionnaire (Cal-AI antipattern in B2B context); story-onboarding doctrine (Mau Baron antipattern in B2B); aggressive paywall at climax (deferred-paywall doctrine wins). Empty-state seeding template content per role: PM → 'Roadmap' database, 'OKRs' page; Engineer → 'Tasks' issues with priority labels; Designer → 'Spec' page with image-blocks; Founder → 'Strategy' notebook; Marketer → 'Campaign' tracker.
real_world_examples: (1) Linear (2021-present, Karri Saarinen): 4-screen onboarding (workspace name → invite teammates [skippable] → role → done). First dashboard has 8-12 example issues with 'Welcome', 'Try linking', 'Try creating', etc. — each example issue IS a coach mark. Time-to-first-issue ~30s. (2) Notion: 3-screen welcome → role chip selection → workspace pre-seeded with templates by role → blank-page invitation. Templates per role: PM gets Roadmap+OKR, Engineer gets Tasks+Sprint, Designer gets Specs+Brand-Kit. (3) Slack: workspace creation → channel auto-creation (#general, #random) → invite teammates as PRIMARY CTA on dashboard, not optional. First-message prompt as floating tooltip. (4) Superhuman: $30/mo, gated behind 30-min Zoom 1:1 with onboarding specialist who walks through keyboard shortcuts. NOT scalable but legendary activation. (5) Cron (now Notion Calendar): account → Google/Apple Calendar OAuth → 90-second keyboard-shortcut tour ('press / to create event') → done. (6) Monday.com: 5-step (workspace → goal → invite → board template → done); invite-a-colleague is step 3 explicitly. (7) Figma: 3-screen (account → first file → 'invite' as floating-action-button on first design). (8) Calendly: 4-step (account → calendar OAuth → availability → share-link). (9) Asana 'Project Templates' onboarding: pre-built templates per use case (marketing campaign, product launch, etc.). (10) Coda role-based templates onboarding identical doctrine to Notion.
mobile_vs_web_diffs: Web-primary for the canonical productivity SaaS (Linear, Notion, Slack, Figma, Asana, Monday — all built web-first). Mobile is companion app with simplified onboarding pulled from web session. Web onboarding leverages keyboard shortcuts, multi-tab, larger viewport. Mobile productivity onboarding (Notion mobile, Slack mobile) is auth-only after web account exists; first-time mobile-only onboarding is rare in this category. Mobile-first productivity exceptions: Things 3 (todo, single-user, 2-screen onboarding), Bear (notes, 2-screen), Drafts (capture, 1-screen). Web equivalent of mobile patterns: deferred permission ladder is keyboard-shortcut tour; magic-link is SSO; biometric gate is browser-stored session.
platform_design_language_alignment: Productivity tooling has its OWN aesthetic conventions (Linear's monochrome ink, Notion's editorial whitespace, Figma's neutral chrome) that PREDATE Liquid Glass / M3E. Liquid Glass alignment: Notion mobile uses Liquid Glass material in its 2025 update; Slack mobile uses translucent toolbars. M3E alignment: Asana, Monday.com on Android use M3E expressive shape system. But the dominant design language in productivity is Sage/Ruler chrome — neutral, opinionated typography (Inter is acceptable here, paradoxically, because Inter's restrained register matches B2B SaaS conventions; Söhne is upgrade), monochrome with single accent (Linear ember orange, Notion no-color minimal), grid-aligned everything. Liquid Glass and M3E are SECONDARY skins on top of this productivity-aesthetic core.
category_fit: Strongest fit: project management (Linear, Asana, Monday, ClickUp, Jira), notes/wiki (Notion, Coda, Confluence, Obsidian), team chat (Slack, Discord-pro, Teams), calendar/scheduling (Cron, Calendly, Reclaim, Motion), email (Superhuman, Hey, Front), design collaboration (Figma, Penpot, Canva), CRM (Pipedrive, Attio, Folk), spreadsheets/dbs (Airtable, Coda), analytics (Amplitude, Mixpanel, PostHog), DevTools (Vercel, Linear, Sentry, GitHub). EXCLUSIONS: B2B with high regulatory friction (HIPAA EHR, financial-services compliance) requires longer auth+verification onboarding. Pure consumer apps falsely categorized as 'productivity' (Habitica, Streaks, Notion-for-personal) should use consumer doctrine.
solo_dev_feasibility: 1_week for minimal frictionless pattern (auth + workspace + role + seeded empty state). 1_month if including SSO infrastructure (WorkOS) + per-role template library + deferred paywall at usage threshold. Productivity is the EASIEST category for solo dev because the doctrine is 'do less' — no AI reveal, no theatrical loader, no 33-step questionnaire to author. Dependencies: Auth (Clerk, Supabase Auth, Stytch, WorkOS for SSO), Stripe/Paddle (per-seat billing — RevenueCat less common in B2B), email (Resend, Postmark for invites), backend (Supabase, Convex, Firebase).
tooling_options: Auth: Clerk (best B2B UX), WorkOS (enterprise SSO), Stytch, Supabase Auth, Auth0 Universal Login. Onboarding-flow tooling LESS COMMON than in consumer (no Adapty/Superwall — billing is Stripe/Paddle). Coach marks: Pendo, Appcues (B2B-leaning), Userpilot, Chameleon, Product Fruits. Analytics: PostHog, Amplitude, Mixpanel, Heap (PLG-leaning). A/B testing: Eppo, ConfigCat, LaunchDarkly. Email: Resend, Postmark. Empty-state seeding: handcoded usually; some teams use Liveblocks/Convex for collaborative seed. Server-driven UI rare in B2B (less ROI on iteration).
ai_dependency: none for the core flow. Optional: AI-powered template-recommendation by role (e.g., Notion AI suggesting templates based on workspace name) — small classifier model sufficient. Optional: AI workspace-bootstrapping (e.g., 'Describe your team' → AI generates initial issue list) — frontier model, but adds latency and isn't load-bearing. The Cal-AI-style AI plan reveal is OUT OF DOCTRINE in B2B because productivity users perceive theatrical AI as gimmicky.
claude_design_application: Prompt fragment: 'Build a productivity SaaS onboarding FSM in XState v5 with 4 steps: AUTH (magic link or SSO via Clerk/WorkOS — NO password), WORKSPACE (name + URL slug), ROLE (4-7 chips: PM/Designer/Engineer/Founder/Marketer/Other — used for empty-state SEEDING, NOT identity formation), DONE (land on dashboard with role-seeded example content). Total 4 screens, target time-to-first-action <60 seconds. The dashboard MUST come pre-populated with example issues/docs/blocks per role. Invite-a-teammate prompt is DEFERRED to after first user-action (item 21, opinionated empty state). NO paywall at onboarding — gate at usage threshold (e.g., 250 issues, 5 editors). NO theatrical AI reveal. NO 33-step questionnaire. Voice: Sage/Ruler — confident, informational, NOT gentle. Forbid: questionnaire-heavy openings; identity formation copy; consumer-wellness pacing; gradient-heavy hero on auth screen (B2B users associate with low-trust); Inter-only stack (use Söhne or Geist for distinction).' Second fragment: 'Build the role-seeded empty state. PM role: pre-create a "Roadmap" database with 4 example issues (Q1-Q4). Engineer role: pre-create a "Sprint" project with 6 example tasks. Designer: pre-create a "Spec" doc with 3 image-block placeholders. Founder: pre-create a "Strategy" notebook. Marketer: pre-create a "Campaign" tracker. Each example artifact contains contextual coach marks ("Click to edit", "Drag to reorder") that disappear after first interaction.'
stitch_application: Prompt fragment: 'Mockup a 4-step productivity SaaS onboarding. Step 1 AUTH — single-screen magic-link form, brand mark top-left, headline "Sign in to [App]" 32pt Söhne weight 500, single email field, primary button "Send magic link" 14pt, secondary text-link "Sign in with Google". White background, ink-color text, single accent (ember orange). NO gradient, NO 3D illustration, NO emoji. Step 2 WORKSPACE — "Name your workspace" text field, URL slug auto-derived. Step 3 ROLE — "What''s your role?" 6 chips arranged 3×2 (PM, Designer, Engineer, Founder, Marketer, Other). Step 4 DASHBOARD (post-onboarding) — landing in fully-seeded workspace by role, example issues visible, single floating-action-button "Invite teammates" in top-right (deferred prompt). Forbid: Inter font (use Söhne/Geist), indigo-500 (use brand ember orange), purple-blue gradient anywhere, 3-column feature grid, emoji bullets, story-arc narrative typography. The aesthetic is Linear/Notion-leaning — restrained, opinionated, confident, monochrome with single accent.'
ai_codegen_failure_mode: AI typically (1) builds a 10-15 step onboarding by default (over-corrects from consumer-wellness training data), (2) adds an AI-plan-reveal screen ('Generate your roadmap with AI!') that reads as gimmicky in B2B context, (3) defaults to indigo-500 + purple-blue gradient on auth screen (signaling consumer-app vibe to B2B users), (4) requires password creation by default (2025-2026 default is magic link + SSO), (5) skips the role-seeded empty state and lands user on a blank dashboard with a generic 'Welcome' modal, (6) places paywall AT onboarding instead of deferred at usage threshold, (7) frames invite as required step instead of deferred prompt. Counter-prompt: 'Build a productivity SaaS onboarding with EXACTLY 4 steps (auth, workspace, role, done). NO password — magic link or SSO only. NO AI plan reveal. NO 33-step questionnaire. NO paywall at onboarding — defer to usage threshold. The dashboard MUST come pre-seeded with role-specific example content. Invite is a DEFERRED floating-action prompt, not a required step. Voice is Sage/Ruler confident, NOT gentle/Caregiver. Use Söhne or Geist (forbid Inter). Brand color in OKLCH, single accent. NO gradient anywhere, NO emoji bullets, NO three-column feature grid.'
anti_patterns: (1) 33-step questionnaire (Cal-AI doctrine in B2B context — kills D1 retention). (2) Story-onboarding (Mau-Baron doctrine — feels manipulative in B2B). (3) AI plan reveal at climax (gimmicky in productivity context). (4) Paywall at onboarding (defer to usage threshold; per-seat billing breaks consumer paywall framing). (5) Blank empty state without seeding (canonical B2B activation killer). (6) Required invite-a-teammate as a step (block-the-flow antipattern). (7) Password-required auth (2025-2026 default is magic link + SSO). (8) Coach-mark deck-of-cards tour at first dashboard (taxes working memory; YouTube Android contextual coach marks are correct pattern). (9) Consumer-wellness Caregiver register in B2B copy ('We're so glad you're here!'). (10) Toggle paywall (banned 2026-01) — moot in B2B since paywalls are deferred. (11) Generic Lucide icon trinity (Box/Check/Zap) on first dashboard signaling AI slop. (12) Color-saturated Liquid-Glass-overdone aesthetic — productivity expects restraint.
regulatory_risk: Lower than consumer-wellness in most jurisdictions, but specific risks: (a) GDPR/CCPA — workspace data, team-member emails, SSO claims constitute personal data; data-processing-agreement (DPA) required for B2B SaaS at scale. (b) SOC 2 Type II expected by enterprise buyers (audit infra). (c) HIPAA if handling protected health info (specific verticals — medical workspaces). (d) FERPA if education customer (Canvas, Schoology). (e) FTC Click-to-Cancel and CA AB 2863 apply to B2B SaaS subscriptions; per-seat cancellation must be self-service. (f) EU Digital Fairness Act less relevant (B2B exemptions in many provisions, though not universal). (g) State-level (NY SHIELD Act, CA SB 220) data breach notification.
platform_constraints_2026: Apple Guideline 3.1.1 (IAP) — B2B SaaS commonly uses external billing (Stripe) on web, exempt from IAP if SaaS-pure. Apple Guideline 3.1.3(b) — Reader Rule allows external account access without IAP for productivity SaaS. Apple Guideline 4.8 — sign-in alternatives required (Sign in with Apple if Google/Facebook/Microsoft offered). Apple Guideline 5.1.1(v) — data minimization. Google Play Subscription Policy — same principles apply. ATT (App Tracking Transparency) — limited relevance in B2B but applies if mobile app collects IDFA. Microsoft Entra ID / Google Workspace SSO standards (SAML, OIDC) for enterprise.
accessibility_implications: WCAG 2.2 AA required for B2B (most enterprise procurement requires VPAT). Keyboard-first navigation critical (productivity users keyboard-heavy). Screen-reader compatibility required (employer ADA exposure). Focus indicators (SC 2.4.7) must be visible — Liquid Glass translucent focus rings often violate. Touch target 24×24 px (SC 2.5.8) — common failure on dense data grids (Airtable/Notion mobile). Color contrast 4.5:1 (SC 1.4.3) — Linear's ink-on-cream meets, but ember-orange on light grey often fails. Dragging alternatives (SC 2.5.7, new in 2.2) — drag-to-reorder issues need keyboard alternative. Redundant entry (SC 3.3.7) — workspace setup must not re-ask SSO claims. EU EAA 2025 enforcement applies to enterprise buyers in EU.
pitfalls_caveats: (1) Mistaking productivity onboarding for consumer = 33-step questionnaire kills D1 retention by 50%+. (2) Skipping role-seeded empty state = canonical B2B activation killer (the blank-page abandonment). (3) Deferring invite past D7 = misses network-effect activation window. (4) Per-seat billing requires careful upgrade-path UX (free→trial→paid by seat). (5) SSO infrastructure (WorkOS, Auth0 Enterprise) is expensive — solo dev should start with email magic link + Google OAuth, add SAML later. (6) Solo-dev productivity SaaS often skip the deferred-paywall doctrine because cash flow demands earlier monetization — but this kills D7 retention; better to monetize via free-tier-volume gate (e.g., '3 projects max on free') than paywall-at-onboarding. (7) International expansion: B2B SaaS localization is workspace-language not user-language; underestimated complexity. (8) Mobile app for productivity SaaS often launched without proper deep-link from web invite email; broken activation funnel.
cross_cultural_validity: Strong fit globally for English-language B2B SaaS. JP: B2B onboarding expects business-card formality; informal microcopy reads unprofessional. KR: B2B prefers KakaoWork/Naver-Works integrations; SSO via local providers. CN: market closed for foreign B2B SaaS; native players (Lark by ByteDance, DingTalk, Feishu) dominate. EU: GDPR-strict consent flows; DPA/SOC2 prerequisite. LATAM: B2B SaaS underpenetrated; ARR potential but local-language onboarding required. India: large B2B SaaS market (Freshworks, Zoho); local-language regional support increasingly expected. RTL Arabic: enterprise SAML SSO + RTL workspace UI required for MENA enterprise.
prescribed_action: Use Frictionless / Deferred Pattern (item 19) extended with role-seeding. EXACTLY 4 onboarding steps: AUTH (magic link or SSO, no password), WORKSPACE (name + URL slug), ROLE (chips for empty-state seeding, NOT identity formation), DONE (land on role-seeded dashboard). Time-to-first-meaningful-action <60s. Invite-a-teammate is a DEFERRED prompt after first action, NOT a required step. NO paywall at onboarding — gate at usage threshold post-aha (Linear 250-issues, Notion-style block volume, Figma editor seats). NO 33-step questionnaire (Cal-AI doctrine OUT OF SCOPE). NO story-onboarding theatrics (Mau-Baron doctrine OUT OF SCOPE). NO AI plan reveal at climax. Voice: Sage/Ruler confident-informational, NOT Caregiver gentle. Aesthetic: restrained Linear/Notion-leaning, single accent color, Söhne or Geist (forbid Inter-default), no gradient. Defer permissions, defer invite, defer paywall — onboarding's job is to unblock work, not extract commitment.
questionnaire_relevance: Single role question only (4-7 chips) for empty-state SEEDING, NOT identity formation. Forbid demographic-heavy openings, forbid 33-step extraction. Conversational form (item 28) for the one role question.
aha_relevance: Aha = first-meaningful-action with seeded content. Linear: first issue created in <30s. Notion: first block typed. Slack: first message sent. Figma: first file opened. Time-to-value <60-90 seconds — productivity benchmark. NO theatrical AI reveal — the seeded empty state IS the activation.
telemetry_events: onboarding_step_viewed, onboarding_step_completed, auth_method_used (magic_link/sso/password), workspace_created, role_selected, empty_state_seeded, first_meaningful_action (issue_created/block_typed/message_sent/file_opened), invite_prompted, invite_sent, integration_connected, usage_threshold_reached, paywall_shown_at_threshold, trial_started, paid_conversion.
measurement_horizon: Day_7 workspace retention as primary; D14 invite-conversion; Day_30 usage-threshold-conversion; 12-month per-seat ARR.
emotional_archetype_alignment: PRIMARY: Sage (Linear, Notion — wisdom, opinionated defaults, confident voice), Ruler (Asana, Monday — control, structure, professional). SECONDARY: Creator (Figma, Notion — making things), Hero (Slack, Linear — empowering work). MISMATCHES: Caregiver (gentle pacing reads weak), Magician (theatrical AI reveal reads gimmicky), Outlaw (Linear is Sage-leaning despite Karri Saarinen's design taste), Jester (humor undermines workplace credibility — Slack's Slackbot copy is rare exception, used sparingly).
anti_pattern_paired: Pairs against Long Questionnaire / Sunk-Cost Pattern (item 17) — opposite doctrine in B2B. Pairs against Story-Onboarding Doctrine (item 18) — opposite doctrine. Pairs against No-Escape Room (rigid 12-step unskippable tour), Wall-of-Text + Cognitive Overload, Premature Authentication (workspace creation requires auth, but role question is post-auth — flow is correct), Generic Celebration (mismatched archetype voice).
